sources: GSA (gsacom.com) - State of the Market April 2026 (gsacom.com/paper/state-of-the-market-april-2026/)
OpenSignal - Global Mobile Network Experience Awards 2025, country reports
data_notes: 5G Total: GSA State of the Market April 2026 (data as of end March 2026) — 392 operators have launched 5G networks (up 14% from March 2025), representing 44% of total LTE and 5G networks. 643 operators investing in 5G. Source: gsacom.com/paper/state-of-the-market-april-2026/

SA status: GSA confirms 95 operators have launched 5G Standalone as of March 2026 (up 42% since Q1 2025). GSA per-operator SA list is available to members only (GAMBoD database). Individual operator SA status in this dataset is based on public operator announcements and GSA regional reports. Source: gsacom.com/paper/state-of-the-market-april-2026/

5G Advanced (3GPP Rel-18): GSA April 2026 confirms 11 operators have launched 5G-A service, 35 operators are investing. GSA per-operator 5G-A list is members-only. Operators marked five_g_advanced: true in this dataset are confirmed via public announcements. Source: gsacom.com/paper/state-of-the-market-april-2026/

FWA: GSA April 2026 confirms 248 operators have launched 5G fixed wireless service (29 more investing, +14% since June 2025). Separately, GSA FWA Market June 2026 confirms 514 operators in 179 countries have launched any FWA (LTE or 5G); Europe and ME&A have highest operator counts. Avg 5G FWA peak DL: 774.7 Mbps. Per-operator list is members-only. The fwa field in this dataset marks confirmed 5G FWA operators based on public operator announcements. Source: gsacom.com/paper/state-of-the-market-april-2026/ and gsacom.com/paper/fwa-market-june-2026/

NTN: GSA May 2026 confirms 275 operator-satellite partnerships in 101 countries. 57 operators in 35 countries have launched commercial NTN: 21 satellite-to-cellphone partnerships (17 countries), 23 fixed broadband NTN. Broadband NTN still early commercial stage. Source: gsacom.com/press-release/275-ntn-partnerships/
Speed scores: OpenSignal figures = median speeds from user measurements; Ookla = median from Speedtest app; marked as ESTIMATES where exact published figure unavailable

GSMA vs GSA: GSMA counts 383 commercial 5G operators in 146 countries; GSA counts 392 launched networks (March 2026). Difference is methodology — GSMA and GSA use different definitions for 'launched' (GSA more conservative on city-pilots)

global_stats.note: GSA Public Mobile Networks Dec 2025 (data Nov 2025): 374 operators in 143 countries launched commercial 5G, 86 with SA. April 2026 (data Mar 2026): 392 operators, 95 SA. Per-operator details are in the GSA GAMBoD database (members only).

operators.name: SK Telecom
operators.five_g_advanced_note: No confirmed commercial 5G-A launch. SA not yet deployed commercially as of June 2026.
operators.fwa_note: 5G FWA confirmed from public operator announcements; cross-referenced with GSA FWA Market June 2026 (514 operators in 179 countries)
operators.sa_launch_note: South Korean government mandates SA upgrades by 2026; SK Telecom targets Q4 2026 commercial SA. B2B-only private SA at SK Hynix plant only. (Source: THELEC, fierce-network 2026)

operators.name: KT Corporation

opensignal.note: KT and LG U+ surpassing 430 Mbps per OpenSignal June 2025

operators.fwa_note: 5G FWA confirmed from public operator announcements; cross-referenced with GSA FWA Market June 2026 (514 operators in 179 countries)
operators.five_g_advanced_note: KT has SA since July 2021 but no confirmed commercial 5G-A (Rel-18) service announcement found as of June 2026.
operators.sa_launch_note: Samsung and KT launched Korea's first commercial 5G SA on 2021-07-15 (Source: Samsung Global Newsroom, RCRwireless). In March 2026, KT launched 5G SA for iPhone users.

operators.name: LG U+
operators.sa_note: SA deployment planned but not commercially launched as of Dec 2025
operators.five_g_advanced_note: LG U+ has not launched 5G SA (still NSA, 4G core). South Korean mandate requires SA by 2026. Cannot claim 5G-A without SA core. (Source: fierce-network 2026)

operators.name: T-Mobile USA
operators.sa_note: First US operator to launch SA nationwide (600 MHz Aug 2020); mid-band SA added Nov 2022
operators.ntn_note: SpaceX Starlink satellite-to-mobile partnership for SMS/voice coverage extension. NOT 3GPP NTN broadband.
operators.five_g_advanced_note: T-Mobile confirmed multiple 3GPP Rel-18 (5G-A) features launching in 2026; among GSA-confirmed 11 operators globally with launched 5G-A services as of Q1 2026.

opensignal.note: OpenSignal USA June 2025: T-Mobile leads US overall DL 177.5 Mbps, 5G DL 252.4 Mbps

operators.fwa_note: 5G FWA confirmed from public operator announcements; cross-referenced with GSA FWA Market June 2026 (514 operators in 179 countries)

operators.name: Verizon
operators.launch_note: Oct 2018 was FWA-only mmWave launch in 4 cities; mobile 5G launched April 2019
operators.sa_note: SA core switched on autumn 2025; nationwide but still expanding SA site count. Previously NSA only.
operators.fwa_note: One of the largest 5G FWA providers in the US; 14.6M combined FWA subs with AT&T and T-Mobile as of Q3 2025
operators.ntn_note: No NTN partnership announced as of mid-2026
operators.five_g_advanced_note: 5G-A rollout expected 2026 once SA deployment matures

opensignal.note: OpenSignal USA June 2025; lower overall due to mmWave-heavy strategy with limited coverage

operators.name: AT&T
operators.sa_note: AT&T announced nationwide 5G SA in October 2025, built on Azure cloud core with 60+ containerized network functions. Previously NSA only.
operators.fwa_note: RedCap nationwide rollout July 2025; combined FWA subs growing with Verizon and T-Mobile to 14.6M as of Q3 2025
operators.five_g_advanced_note: 5G-A network slices planned for 2026 per AT&T announcements

operators.name: China Mobile
operators.sa_note: China launched 5G as SA from day one; no NSA phase
operators.ntn_note: China Mobile has satellite IoT NTN services via its own satellite constellation. Broadband NTN not yet commercial.
operators.five_g_advanced_note: China Mobile deploying Rel-18 features and has promoted 5G-A branding since 2024; among GSA-confirmed 11 operators globally with launched 5G-A services (Q1 2026).

opensignal.note: OpenSignal does not publish China operator-level breakdowns publicly; figure is estimate based on GSMA/industry reports

operators.fwa_note: 5G FWA confirmed from public operator announcements; cross-referenced with GSA FWA Market June 2026 (514 operators in 179 countries)

operators.name: China Telecom
